actions.name: AcknowledgeAlert
actions.description: Acknowledges an open Alert, marking it as seen and assigning ownership to the acknowledging user. Updates the alert status to 'ack', records the timestamp, and sets the assigned_to field.

alert_id.description: The primary key of the Alert to acknowledge.

acknowledged_by.description: Username or user ID of the person acknowledging the alert.

notes.description: Optional free-text notes describing the acknowledgement context or initial triage findings.

preconditions.condition: Alert with alert_id exists in the ontology
preconditions.error_if_false: Alert not found: no Alert object exists with the provided alert_id.
Alert cannot be acknowledged: current status is not 'open'. Only open alerts may be acknowledged.

postconditions.guarantee: Alert.acknowledged_at is set to the current UTC timestamp at the time of action execution.
Alert.assigned_to is set to the value of acknowledged_by.

steps: Clear Alert.acknowledged_at by setting it to null.
Clear Alert.assigned_to by setting it to null.

actions.name: CreateIncident
actions.description: Creates a new Incident record grouping one or more alerts into an operational event. Links any provided Alerts to the new Incident, sets initial status to 'open', and returns the new incident_id.

title.description: A short, descriptive title for the incident.

severity.description: Severity level of the incident. P1 is most critical.

affected_systems.description: List of system names or identifiers impacted by this incident.

alert_ids.description: Optional list of Alert IDs to link to this incident as triggering alerts.

created_by.description: Username or user ID of the person creating the incident.

preconditions.error_if_false: Incident title must be a non-empty string.
affected_systems must contain at least one system identifier.
severity must be a valid enum value: P1, P2, P3, or P4.

postconditions.guarantee: A new Incident object is created with status='open' and the provided title, severity, and affected_systems.
Each Alert in alert_ids (if provided) is linked to the new Incident via the alert_triggers_incident link type.
The newly generated incident_id is returned to the caller.

steps: Delete the newly created Incident record from the ontology.
Remove all alert_triggers_incident links between the Alerts in alert_ids and the deleted Incident.
Restore each linked Alert's status to its pre-action value if it was modified during incident creation.

actions.name: PromoteModel
actions.description: Promotes a ModelVersion to a higher lifecycle status (training→staging or staging→production). Validates evaluation scores and state transition rules. Archives any existing production model when promoting to production.

model_id.description: The primary key of the ModelVersion to promote.

target_status.description: The target lifecycle status for this promotion.

promoted_by.description: Username or user ID of the person authorizing the promotion.

justification.description: Mandatory written justification describing why the model is ready for promotion.

preconditions.condition: ModelVersion with model_id exists in the ontology
preconditions.error_if_false: ModelVersion not found: no ModelVersion object exists with the provided model_id.

preconditions.condition: State transition is valid: current status must be 'training' when promoting to 'staging', or 'staging' when promoting to 'production'
preconditions.error_if_false: Invalid state transition: model can only be promoted from training→staging or staging→production.

preconditions.condition: At least one EvaluationResult linked to this model_id exists in the ontology
preconditions.error_if_false: Promotion blocked: no EvaluationResults found for this ModelVersion. At least one evaluation must be completed before promotion.

preconditions.condition: All linked EvaluationResult scores meet the minimum threshold defined for the target_status
preconditions.error_if_false: Promotion blocked: one or more evaluation scores do not meet the required threshold for the target status.

postconditions.guarantee: ModelVersion.promoted_at is set to the current UTC timestamp.
If target_status is 'production', any existing ModelVersion with status='production' for the same base_architecture is set to status='archived'.

steps: Restore ModelVersion.status to its pre-action value.
Clear ModelVersion.promoted_at by setting it to null.
If a previously production model was archived during this action, restore its status back to 'production'.

actions.name: TriggerPipelineRun
actions.description: Triggers an immediate execution of an active DataPipeline. Validates the pipeline is not already running and optionally accepts a config override as JSON. Returns the new run_id for tracking.

pipeline_id.description: The primary key of the DataPipeline to trigger.

triggered_by.description: Username or user ID of the person triggering the pipeline run.

override_config_json.description: Optional JSON string with configuration overrides that supersede the pipeline's default config for this run only.

preconditions.condition: DataPipeline with pipeline_id exists in the ontology
preconditions.error_if_false: DataPipeline not found: no DataPipeline object exists with the provided pipeline_id.
Pipeline cannot be triggered: current status is not 'active'. Only active pipelines may be triggered.
Pipeline cannot be triggered: a run is already in progress (last_run_status is 'running').

postconditions.guarantee: DataPipeline.last_run_ts is set to the current UTC timestamp.
A new run_id is generated and returned to the caller for tracking the pipeline execution.

steps: Set DataPipeline.last_run_status back to its pre-action value.
Restore DataPipeline.last_run_ts to its pre-action value.
Cancel any downstream jobs or compute tasks that were dispatched as part of this triggered run.

actions.name: FlagDataQualityIssue
actions.description: Flags a data quality problem on a Dataset by creating or updating a DataQualityReport. Increments the anomaly count, records the issue details, and triggers an Alert if severity is critical.

dataset_rid.description: The primary key of the Dataset with the quality issue.

issue_type.description: The category of data quality issue being reported.

description.description: Human-readable description of the specific quality issue observed.

flagged_by.description: Username or user ID of the person or system flagging the issue.

severity.description: Severity level of the data quality issue. 'critical' issues automatically trigger an Alert.

preconditions.condition: Dataset with dataset_rid exists in the ontology
preconditions.error_if_false: Dataset not found: no Dataset object exists with the provided dataset_rid.
issue_type must be a valid enum value: completeness, uniqueness, validity, or anomaly.

postconditions.guarantee: A DataQualityReport is created for the dataset_rid if one does not already exist for the current reporting period; if one exists, its anomaly_count is incremented by 1 and issues_json is updated with the new issue entry.
Dataset.last_updated_at is set to the current UTC timestamp.

If severity is 'critical', a new Alert object is created with severity='P1', status='open', source_system='data-quality', and a raw_payload_json summarizing the issue, and this Alert is linked to the Dataset via the audit trail.

steps: If a new DataQualityReport was created, delete it. If an existing report was updated, decrement anomaly_count by 1 and remove the issue entry from issues_json.
Restore Dataset.last_updated_at to its pre-action value.
If a critical Alert was created during this action, cancel and delete that Alert object.
